assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Alexey, OrbitFlow has contractors across Armenia, Georgia, Kazakhstan — with payments going through Wise and crypto plus a lot of manual coordination. Mellow removes that friction: automatic payouts, real-time statuses, no more chasing status updates. Quick question: what's eating the most ops time right now?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.
criteria.evidence_quote: The current setup stops being defensible in two scenarios: a payment gets stuck with no SLA path — or diligence comes and there's no audit trail. Your current setup has both risks. Mellow removes them specifically.
criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

criteria.name: Language fit
criteria.short_reason: Language is adapted to the chosen persona.

Mellow removes that friction: automatic payouts, real-time statuses, no more chasing status updates. Quick question: what's eating the most ops time right now?
criteria.label: Persona language fit

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: Alexey, OrbitFlow has contractors across Armenia, Georgia, Kazakhstan — with payments going through Wise and crypto plus a lot of manual coordination. Mellow removes that friction: automatic payouts, real-time statuses, no more chasing status updates. Quick question: what's eating the most ops time right now?

objections.objection: Ok but crypto + Wise has worked for two years. Why do I need this specifically now?
Send me the flow on one screen. What specifically stops being manual, how much time that saves.
The current setup is slow but I understand it. What specifically becomes more predictable?
Send it. If it's concrete — I'll respond.

criteria.label: Core objection handling

transcript.text: Alexey, OrbitFlow has contractors across Armenia, Georgia, Kazakhstan — with payments going through Wise and crypto plus a lot of manual coordination. Mellow removes that friction: automatic payouts, real-time statuses, no more chasing status updates. Quick question: what's eating the most ops time right now?
Ok but crypto + Wise has worked for two years. Why do I need this specifically now?

The current setup stops being defensible in two scenarios: a payment gets stuck with no SLA path — or diligence comes and there's no audit trail. Your current setup has both risks. Mellow removes them specifically.
Send me the flow on one screen. What specifically stops being manual, how much time that saves.
Concretely for ops: less manual status chasing, less reconciliation overhead, and a named owner for incidents. That is the before/after change, not a generic platform claim.
The current setup is slow but I understand it. What specifically becomes more predictable?

Concretely for ops: less manual status chasing, less reconciliation overhead, and a named owner for incidents. That is the before/after change, not a generic platform claim. What would take the most pain off your team specifically?
Send it. If it's concrete — I'll respond.
Concretely for ops: less manual status chasing, less reconciliation overhead, and a named owner for incidents. That is the before/after change, not a generic platform claim.
Specifically: what stops being manual? Give me hours and steps, not marketing language.
